success_surface: Conditional cell updates with mixed value and format operations
effective_behaviors: Explored spreadsheet structure programmatically before implementing changes
Strictly respected the specified answer position range for row iteration
Applied case-insensitive partial string matching for text search
Validated numeric data types before performing whole-number detection
Clearing cells removed both values and associated styling/formatting
Preserved file integrity by writing to output path rather than modifying source
Logged execution summary with affected operations and ranges
evidence: Agent ran inspection code to print sheet names, column headers, data in target range, and column H formatting before solving
Iteration used range(2, 18) exactly matching the E2:E17 answer position constraint
String check used str(e_value).lower() and 'georgia its tax' in ... to handle case and partial matches
Added isinstance(f_value, (int, float)) guard before comparing f_value == int(f_value)
Column H clearing used PatternFill(start_color=None, end_color=None, fill_type=None) alongside setting value to None
Output saved to distinct output_path instead of overwriting input_path
Print statements reported changes made including columns affected and row ranges processed

memory_items.title: Pre-modification Structure Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect workbook structure before implementing changes
memory_items.generalization: When manipulating spreadsheets, run discovery code to list sheet names, verify column positions, examine sample data values, and check existing formatting in target areas before writing transformation logic.

memory_items.title: Answer Position Constraint Adherence
memory_items.lesson: Respect the exact row/column bounds from answer position specifications
memory_items.generalization: Use the provided answer_position range strictly for iterations—convert cell references to programmatic indices without exceeding the minimum required rows or columns.

memory_items.title: Robust String Matching for Conditional Logic
memory_items.lesson: Handle text searches with case normalization and partial matching
memory_items.generalization: When searching for specific text values, convert cell content to lowercase strings and use substring containment checks rather than exact equality to accommodate real-world variations.

memory_items.title: Type-Safe Numeric Comparisons
memory_items.lesson: Validate data types before performing numeric operations
memory_items.generalization: Check if a value is actually numeric (int or float) before applying numeric predicates like whole-number detection to avoid errors on empty or text cells.

memory_items.title: Complete Cell Clearing Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Removing content requires clearing both values and visual styling
memory_items.generalization: When instructed to make cells empty, reset both cell.value and cell.fill attributes—not just one—to fully comply with format-free requirements.

memory_items.title: Execution Logging and Summary
memory_items.lesson: Document what changes were made after successful execution
memory_items.generalization: After processing, output a summary report stating which columns were modified, how many rows were affected, and confirmation that all requested operations completed.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Pre-Modification Verification
- Always inspect workbook structure before implementing changes: list available sheets, verify column positions by header name, examine sample data values in the target range, and check existing formatting.

### Scope Boundary Control
- Respect the exact row/column bounds specified in answer_position constraints; do not iterate beyond the minimum required range even if data exists elsewhere.

### Robust Text Search Patterns
- For text-based conditions, normalize cell values to lowercase strings and use substring containment checks rather than exact equality to handle case variations and partial matches.

### Type Validation Before Numeric Operations
- Guard numeric comparisons with type checks (`isinstance(value, (int, float))`) before evaluating mathematical properties like whole-number detection to prevent errors on empty or non-numeric cells.

### Complete Cell Reset Protocol
- When clearing cells per instructions, explicitly reset both `cell.value = None` and `cell.fill` attributes to ensure all content and styling are removed.

### Execution Transparency
- After successful processing, log a summary report stating which columns were modified, the row ranges affected, and confirmation that all requested operations completed successfully.
